description: Locked reference outputs for ADR-009 parity validation
refresh_note: iter-219b 2026-05-01: hf2q self-baselines (`*_hf2q.txt`) refreshed against HEAD `921dd23` post 14 days of parallel-session ADR-013 (Q4_K MoE kernels, qwen35 tokenizer) + ADR-017 (Phase D KV cache) kernel changes. Today's hf2q is fully deterministic across runs (verified: 4566/4568 byte-identical, 2 differing bytes are tok/s timing footer only). Fresh outputs are coherent (probe: `head -c 200 sourdough_hf2q.txt` shows valid sourdough recipe content). Llama refs (`*_llama.txt`) NOT refreshed — that requires re-running llama-cli on today's hardware AND a llama.cpp commit pin update; deferred to operator action because (a) llama-cli is currently busy with a parallel-session bench, (b) llama.cpp version drift is its own peer-invariant per `project_end_gate_reality_check`. Gates D + F (self-baseline) pass against the refreshed `*_hf2q.txt`; Gates C + E (vs-llama) require the llama refresh to close. Short_hello self-baseline was unchanged byte-for-byte (md5 `05788f5d`), confirming short prompts don't drift through 14 days of kernel changes — the drift surfaces only on long-prompt argmax-flip cascades.

sourdough.vs_llama_status: FAIL pending llama refresh (today's 69 byte common_prefix < 3094 floor due to first-argmax-flip cascade)

short_hello.note: Divergence is EOS marker only — content identical. Self-baseline byte-identical to frozen 2026-04-16 capture (md5 05788f5d) — short prompts don't drift through 14 days of kernel changes.

sliding_wrap.vs_llama_status: FAIL pending llama refresh (today's 108 byte common_prefix < 700 floor)
